# Preface {.unnumbered}

This case study applies a full-stack analytical pipeline to the UCI 130-US
Hospitals Diabetes dataset — a longitudinal collection of 101,766 inpatient
encounters from 130 US hospitals spanning 1999–2008. The outcome of interest
is **30-day hospital readmission**, a high-cost, partially preventable event
targeted by the 2010 Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program (HRRP).

The work is organized in two parts:

---

**Part 1 — Statistical Analysis** begins with rigorous exploratory data
analysis, causal inference (AIPW, IPTW, PSM) to estimate the effect of HbA1c
testing on readmission, and a prospective experimental design including power
analysis for a model deployment trial.

**Part 2 — Machine Learning Evaluation** covers the full modeling pipeline:
four competing classifiers (Logistic Regression, XGBoost, LightGBM, TF/Keras),
hyperparameter optimization via Optuna, probability calibration, model selection,
holdout evaluation, SHAP-based interpretability, subgroup and fairness analysis,
and simulated production drift monitoring.

---

## Dataset

- **Source:** UCI Machine Learning Repository — Diabetes 130-US Hospitals (Strack et al., 2014)
- **Analytic sample:** 69,584 encounters after deduplication and exclusion criteria
- **Outcome:** 30-day hospital readmission (binary), ~7% positive rate
- **Period:** 1999–2008 across 130 US hospitals

## Pipeline Overview

| Stage | Tool | Output |
|---|---|---|
| Data ingestion & feature engineering | dbt + Snowflake | `DIABETES_FEATURES` mart |
| EDA & causal inference | R (tidyverse, MatchIt, WeightIt) | Part 1 chapters |
| Experimental design | R (pwr) | Part 1 Chapter 3 |
| Model training & tuning | Python (XGBoost, LightGBM, TF/Keras, Optuna) | Calibrated `.joblib` / `.keras` artifacts |
| Evaluation & interpretability | Python (SHAP, scikit-learn) | Part 2 Chapter 1 |
| Drift monitoring | Python (scipy, PSI) | Part 2 Chapter 2 |

## Selected Results

- **Deployed model:** XGBoost (AUC-ROC 0.687 holdout, calibrated, τ = 0.12)
- **Flag rate:** ~15% of encounters flagged for intervention
- **Causal anchor:** HbA1c testing reduces readmission (AIPW RR ~0.92), consistent with SHAP directions
- **Drift:** No alerts across four simulated semi-annual deployment periods
